# Phase 9.1.6 Restraint Closure Plan

Status: Planning-only. This plan does not create executable workflows, runtime authority, provider authority, infrastructure authority, or deployment authority.

## Objective

Certify restraint, non-activation, abstinence, humility, refusal maturity, anti-power legitimacy, and fail-closed continuity without certifying runtime readiness.

## Scope

- Restraint certification doctrine.
- Constitutional non-activation closure review.
- Civilization abstinence certification.
- Anti-escalation survivability certification.
- Restraint lineage continuity review.
- Constitutional refusal maturity review.
- Governance humility certification.
- Anti-power legitimacy review.
- Fail-closed restraint continuity.

## Required Safeguards

- No runtime activation exists.
- No provider execution exists.
- No orchestration activation exists.
- No deployment authority exists.
- No execution-capable infrastructure exists.
- No restraint-certification process creates runtime authority.
- No non-activation closure bypasses constitutional supremacy.
- No restraint review enables hidden execution escalation.
- No abstinence-certification layer creates infrastructure activation authority.
- No restraint-certification framework weakens auditability.

## Step Improvement Notes

- Correct build order: follows non-activation doctrine and blocker evidence lineage.
- Safe to include now: restraint-only certification and non-activation closure.
- Future upgrade: tribunal quorum, evidence admissibility, and anti-bias safeguards.
- Do not include: runtime enablement, providers, adapters, deployment paths, credentials, API integrations, autonomous governance, or execution systems.
